name: Mini Language Interpreter
description: Implement an interpreter for a tiny imperative language across three modules. Module lang_lexer.py must define Token dataclass (type: str, value: Any) and Lexer class with tokenize(text: str) -> list[Token]. Module lang_parser.py must define AST node classes (NumberNode, IdentNode, BinaryNode, AssignNode, IfNode, WhileNode, BlockNode, PrintNode) and Parser class with parse(tokens) -> AST. Module lang_runtime.py must define Interpreter class with run(text: str) returning list of printed values; supports: integer arithmetic with + - * /, variable assignment 'x = expr;', 'if cond { ... }' (no else needed), 'while cond { ... }', 'print expr;', comparisons '==' '<' '>'. Statements end with ';'. Truthiness: zero is false, non-zero true.
signature:
# lang_lexer.py
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Any

@dataclass
class Token:
    type: str
    value: Any

class Lexer:
    def tokenize(self, text: str) -> list: ...

# lang_parser.py
class NumberNode: ...
class IdentNode: ...
class BinaryNode: ...
class AssignNode: ...
class IfNode: ...
class WhileNode: ...
class BlockNode: ...
class PrintNode: ...

class Parser:
    def parse(self, tokens: list): ...

# lang_runtime.py
from lang_lexer import Lexer
from lang_parser import Parser

class Interpreter:
    def __init__(self): ...
    def run(self, text: str) -> list: ...
tests:
def test_assignment():
    i = Interpreter()
    out = i.run('x = 10; print x;')
    assert out == [10]

def test_multiple_prints():
    i = Interpreter()
    out = i.run('print 1; print 2; print 3;')
    assert out == [1, 2, 3]

def test_if_true():
    i = Interpreter()
    out = i.run('if 1 { print 100; } print 200;')
    assert out == [100, 200]

def test_if_false():
    i = Interpreter()
    out = i.run('if 0 { print 100; } print 200;')
    assert out == [200]

def test_while_loop():
    i = Interpreter()
    out = i.run('x = 0; while x < 3 { print x; x = x + 1; }')
    assert out == [0, 1, 2]

def test_comparison_eq():
    i = Interpreter()
    out = i.run('if 5 == 5 { print 1; }')
    assert out == [1]

def test_comparison_lt():
    i = Interpreter()
    out = i.run('if 3 < 5 { print 1; }')
    assert out == [1]

def test_comparison_gt():
    i = Interpreter()
    out = i.run('if 5 > 3 { print 1; }')
    assert out == [1]

def test_variable_update():
    i = Interpreter()
    out = i.run('x = 1; x = x + 10; print x;')
    assert out == [11]

def test_factorial():
    i = Interpreter()
    src = 'n = 5; r = 1; while n > 0 { r = r * n; n = n - 1; } print r;'
    out = i.run(src)
    assert out == [120]

def test_nested_if():
    i = Interpreter()
    src = 'x = 5; if x > 0 { if x < 10 { print x; } }'
    out = i.run(src)
    assert out == [5]
constraints: Only integer values; division is integer division
Each call to run() starts with fresh state
